channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Deadly violence in Nigeria linked to breakup of United Methodist Church over LGBTQ policies
item.description: United Methodist News Service reports that a religious schism has turned deadly in Nigeria, with a church member fatally shot and two young children killed as homes were set ablaze.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Brazilian Santa swaps sleigh for Jet Ski to deliver toys — and joy — to disabled children in Rio
item.description: Dozens of children with disability gathered on Rio de Janeiro’s Copacabana beach on Tuesday to receive presents — and joy — from the white-bearded man, a joint effort by the city’s firefighters and nonprofit associations.
item.dc:creator: Lucas Dumphreys, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Netanyahu says Israeli troops will occupy a buffer zone inside Syria for the foreseeable future
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Tuesday that Israeli forces will stay in a buffer zone on the Syrian border, seized after the ouster of Syria's President Bashar Assad, until another arrangement is in place “that ensures Israel's security.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: More world leaders from Argentina and El Salvador invited to Trump's inauguration
item.description: More world leaders are confirming they have been invited to President-elect Donald Trump’s inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Adriana Gomez Licon And Debora Rey, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Former Church of England leader resigns as priest after claims he mishandled an abuse case
item.description: A former leader of the Church of England has resigned as a priest following allegations that he failed to properly handle the case of a priest accused of sexual misconduct.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bush, Obama alumni ask Biden administration to ease up on Cuba before Trump takes office
item.description: A group of former diplomatic and national security officials is calling on the Biden administration to loosen some restrictions on Cuba before handing over the reins of U.S. diplomacy to President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel plans an extended occupation of Syrian buffer zone
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Israeli troops will occupy a buffer zone inside Syria for the foreseeable future.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada's prime minister faces calls to resign. Here's what could happen next
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau faces growing pressure to resign from his own Liberal Party after his top minister abruptly quit and criticized his handling of the budget.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Roadside bomb kills 3 police officers escorting polio workers in northwest Pakistan
item.description: Pakistan's police say a roadside bomb has exploded near a vehicle carrying police officers assigned to protect polio workers in northwest Pakistan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Iran pauses the process to implement a new, stricter headscarf law for women, official says
item.description: TEHRAN, Iran (AP) — Iran has paused the process of implementing a new, stricter law on women’s mandatory headscarf, or hijab, an official said — a bill that many believe could have reignited the protests that engulfed the Islamic Republic after the 2022 death of Mahsa Amini.
item.dc:creator: AMIR VAHDAT and NASSER KARIMI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Van Hollen, Graham threaten sanctions on Turkey to achieve ceasefire with Syrian Kurdish allies
item.description: Sens. Chris Van Hollen (D-Md.) and Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.) are threatening sanctions against Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan unless Ankara forces Syrian opposition fighters into a ceasefire with U.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Netanyahu says Israel will occupy border territory in Syria for now
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Tuesday that his forces will for now occupy territory they had seized over the border in Syria.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: France rushes aid to Mayotte after Cyclone Chido leaves hundreds feared dead
item.description: MAMOUDZOU, Mayotte (AP) — France used ships and military aircraft to rush rescuers and supplies to Mayotte on Monday after the tiny French island territory off Africa was battered by its worst cyclone in nearly a century. Authorities fear hundreds and possibly thousands of people have died.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, THOMAS ADAMSON and RAINAT ALILOIFFA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Russian opposition figure says fears of his mother's poisoning in Berlin proved false
item.description: A prominent Russian opposition politician freed as part of the largest East-West civilian prisoner swap since the Cold War says that fears of his mother’s poisoning in Berlin have proven false.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Filipina who won a last-minute reprieve from an Indonesian firing squad is returning home
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Indonesian authorities have returned a Filipina woman to the Philippines who had been on death row and nearly executed by firing squad in Indonesia in 2015, after a longstanding request from her home country.
item.dc:creator: NINIEK KARMINI and ANDI JATMIKO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: 2 charged in deadly drone attack on US soldiers in Jordan
item.description: The U.S. on Monday charged two Iranian officials in a scheme that contributed to a deadly January drone attack that killed three American service members in Jordan. The Department of Justice (DOJ) charged Mahdi Mohammad Sadeghi, 42, who is a U.S.-Iranian national from Natick, Mass.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Netanyahu spokesperson denies Cairo trip amid Gaza ceasefire push
item.description: A spokesperson for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Tuesday rejected reports the Israeli leader was traveling to Cairo for negotiations over a ceasefire and hostage release deal with Hamas in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Grassroots funding for Ukraine's military declines as groups looks for creative ways to raise money
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — The concert took place in an old movie studio in Kyiv, the location kept secret until the final moment in case it was the target of a Russian attack.
item.dc:creator: YEHOR KONOVALOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: German Chancellor Olaf Scholz loses no-confidence vote, election called for early next year
item.description: In November, Scholz's government collapsed after the Free Democrat Party quit over the country's debt. An early election to replace Scholz will occur on Feb. 23 following Monday's no-confidence vote.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Palestinian security forces launch a rare crackdown on militants in the West Bank
item.description: JENIN, West Bank (AP) — Palestinian security forces have launched a rare crackdown against local militant groups in the northern West Bank, sending in armored cars and engaging in fierce gunbattles that have killed at least two people in the volatile area.
item.dc:creator: MAJDI MOHAMMED and AREF TUFAHA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian paramilitary soldiers killed in friendly fire attack by North Koreans after enlisting DPRK help
item.description: North Korean troops recently killed Russian soldiers in a friendly fire attack, according to Ukrainian intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cartel-run clandestine fentanyl labs increasingly difficult to track
item.description: Mexican drug cartels are driving an opioid epidemic, flooding the United States with increasingly potent synthetic drugs.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Christian leader in Lebanon urges US, allies to intervene to stop Hezbollah
item.description: The leader of a Christian coalition and political party in Lebanon is calling on the U.S. and its Western allies to deploy troops to Lebanon to help the army dismantle Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea’s parliament votes to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol over his martial law order
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s parliament on Saturday impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol over his stunning and short-lived martial law decree, a move that ended days of political paralysis but set up an intense debate over Yoon's fate, as jubilant crowds roared to celebrate another defiant m
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea lawmakers vote to impeach president over martial law declaration
item.description: South Korean lawmakers on Saturday voted to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol over his short-lived martial law declaration earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Woman arrested smuggling pounds of meth wrapped as a Christmas gift
item.description: Customs officers arrested a traveler who investigators say tried to smuggle pounds of methamphetamine disguised as a Christmas gift into New Zealand last weekend.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Syrians celebrate Assad's fall as US seeks a peaceful political transition
item.description: Thousands of Syrians gathered in Damascus’ main square and a historic mosque for the first Muslim Friday prayers since former President Bashar Assad was overthrown, a major symbolic moment for the country’s dramatic change of power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Saudi Arabia's plans to host the men's World Cup 2034 will be harmful for the climate, experts say
item.description: As the newly-named host of the 2034 World Cup in men's soccer, Saudi Arabia says it will construct or renovate 15 stadiums, create a futuristic city and expand airports in a massive buildout to accommodate millions of athletes, coaches and spectators.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN and JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Capitol rioter who tried to join Russian army is sentenced to prison for probation violation
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A Dallas man who tried to fly overseas to join the Russian military and fight against Ukraine was sentenced on Friday to six months in prison for violating the terms of his probation for storming the U.S. Capitol four years ago. Kevin Loftus, a 56-year-old veteran of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: American freed from Syrian prison after Assad's overthrow taken out of country by US military
item.description: Travis Timmerman, an American who was freed by Syrian rebels this week after the overthrow of former President Bashar al-Assad, has been flown out of the country by the U.S. military.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump invites China's Xi to his inauguration even as he threatens massive tariffs on Beijing
item.description: Asked at a Chinese Foreign Ministry briefing on Thursday about Trump's invitation, spokesperson Mao Ning responded: “I have nothing to share at present.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Coffee prices hit record high after challenging growing season
item.description: Arabica coffee sold for $3.44 a pound on the global market earlier this week, beating the prior record high of $3.35 set in 1977.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel bombs hundreds of sites across Syria as army pushes into border zone
item.description: Israel said Tuesday it had bombed more than 350 military sites in Syria during the previous 48 hours, targeting “most of the strategic weapons stockpiles” in the country.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation
